title: CIVIC ENGAGEMENT AND ADVOCACY - 211info
text: UNITE OREGON WASHINGTON COUNTY CIVIC ENGAGEMENT AND ADVOCACY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Protects and expands the rights of immigrant, refugee, and underserved communities by offering community organizing, policy advocacy, community education, and leadership development. Promotes systemic changes in many issues including affordable housing, fair wages, racial justice, etc. by empowering communities and grassroots organizing. Does not provide direct advocacy for individuals. Eligibility: Immigrant and refugee community members and non-immigrant community members Hours: Varies Email (503) 828-9736 Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers (503) 287-4117 Ext: 5 Main phone Main phone (503) 828-9736 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call or visit website for more information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None LANGUAGES: English, interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: Clackamas, Multnomah, and Washington Counties Services: Ethnic Advocacy Groups Housing Advocacy Groups Human Relations Groups Immigrant Rights Groups Leadership Development * Immigrants If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. UNITE OREGON WASHINGTON COUNTY
